resources.string: A small developer-facing sample that shows the real Katch flow: initialize once, export the key, generate a safe encrypted report, and verify the real crash path.

Katch is initialized in SampleApp. In a real app, you would call Katch.d(), Katch.i(), Katch.w(), and Katch.e() from your own features, then rely on testCrash() or a real uncaught crash to write the report file.
This sample uses an auto-managed encryption key. Copy the key below and pass it to the bundled decryptor when you want to inspect a generated .enc report.
Use these buttons to test the library without searching through the code first.
Generate Test Report writes an encrypted sample report and keeps the app running.
Crash App throws a real uncaught exception so you can verify the crash-handler path end to end.
Auto-managed key enabled. Generated reports will be written as encrypted .enc files.
Auto-managed encryption is enabled, but the exported key is currently unavailable.
No exported key is available to copy
